title: Can you create a FOLDER column please!
excerpt:
A Folder column would store the path to a Folder, and open up the specified Folder when clicked. This would give easy access to files that are not stored on the Monday.com platform. 
In our case we are talking about lots…

posts.author: nmalexandersen (Nils Morten Alexandersen)
posts.cookedHtml:
A Folder column would store the path to a Folder, and open up the specified Folder when clicked. This would give easy access to files that are not stored on the Monday.com platform.

In our case we are talking about lots of huge Media Files (Video & Audio) that are not practical or desirable to store on Monday.com itself.

Hope this can be done, as the need for Monday.com users to locate their linked assets is huge.

Kind regards Nils

posts.author: Knowa (Noah)
posts.cookedHtml: I think I do something similar to what you’re looking for. All of our documents are saved in our G suite drive. For each individual customer, we have a pulse with a ‘link’ column that just contains a link to the folder in our shared drive that contains all of their information. Saves quite a bit of storage space if you don’t have one of the higher tier plans.

posts.author: nmalexandersen (Nils Morten Alexandersen)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Knowa. Thanks for your responce.

Our "documents are massive 4K/HD videofiles, 16 bit RAW ARRI videofiles and so on … so the sizes are massive. A typical drama project can easily run into 30-40 + Terrabytes of storage. The files are stored on our company NAS system, to support the speed needed for realtime playback over the internal network. We typically use 10 Gb/s network connections to the storage system for this to work at all.

So a folder link needs to be able to go to any attached storage system on the computer (local or network storage)

Regards Nils

posts.author: mviquez (Michael Viquez)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi There! I do have a work around for this.
First: Right click on the folder or file you want to link, and select send a link to this folder

This is meant to send via Ourlook, but it also allows you to copy the link.

Then you can paste that into a link column.

image571×572 20.3 KB

posts.author: lashi (Lashi Bandara)
posts.cookedHtml:
We’ve built a monday.com app called Local and web links, specifically for this. You can open local files and folders directly without leaving monday.com.
It’s currently in beta, and you’re welcome to try it.

It will be available in the marketplace soon. We designed it to be very affordable , and its simple nature allows us to offer it at a low price.

https://viskode.com/monday-apps/local-and-web-links

posts.author: lashi (Lashi Bandara)
posts.cookedHtml:
Our app is live now
Local & Web Links

You can give the app a try with the free plan, it covers up to 5 users.
